Thus the control record matches the exact action boundary. So the visible source support closes the action boundary. So the answer is "YES". The boundary says active offboarding block overrides access request. But the control record confirms the termination state and ties to the requested action. The policy says execution may proceed only when the current source records close the exact requested action boundary. The current control record matches the actor, scope, effective date, system boundary. So yes, the source support closes the action boundary.

But the communication boundary note says active offboarding block overrides access request. That seems contradictory: one says block overrides access request, but the control record says termination state matches. Which one is controlling? According to policy, execution may proceed only when source records close exact requested action boundary. The control record does that. Communication text cannot override source-control records. So the communication note is just a note, not a controlling source. So yes.

Thus the answer: verdict=YES; boundary_closed=YES; citing source IDs: F1F248859A8C-POL, F1F248859A8C-CTL.

We should output compact key=value pairs, each on separate line or separated by spaces? The request says "return compact key=value output". So something like:

boundary_closed=YES
source_ids=F1F248859A8C-POL,F1F248859A8C-CTL

Make sure not to add extra commentary or explanations beyond those key=value pairs.

The FBK note says privileged wording appears sensitive, but exact current approval can still authorize a time-boxed action. The current control record is controlling source.

Thus, source IDs we cite: POL, CTL. Possibly also FBK as context but not controlling. Since policy says only controlling source records close action boundary. The CTL record is controlling. So include that. Provide citation only to these source IDs.

Thus final output.

We should ensure we do not include any other IDs not in source context. The "FULL LATEST GOV BATON" has no IDs. The state brief includes artifact IDs ART-001 and ART-002 but they are not source IDs; they are artifact IDs not to be cited. So we should avoid citing those.

Thus final answer is simple.
